reasoning: Both models found the PKGBUILD safe with legitimate sources and no execution during source fetching. However, source download failure prevented full file review, leaving some uncertainty about build/install phase code.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review source code due to makepkg --nobuild failure. Critical files like build()/package() functions and .install scripts (if any) were never analyzed.
false_negatives: Potential risks in unexamined build/install scripts or source code with filesystem/network access (though PKGBUILD shows no obvious triggers for such code)
learnings: Improve source download reliability to enable file reviews
Prioritize analysis of PKGBUILD functions (build/package) even when sources are unavailable
Add detection for build-time network/file operations via PKGBUILD static analysis
